title: Possible god reference Rei is Rei
display_title: Possible god reference Rei is Rei
wikitext:
[]: Possible god-reference: "Rei is Rei"?             Quote Post by V » Sun Oct 12, 2008 5:41 pm This is a thing we had in LOTR fandom: at one point Goldberry is asked who or what Tom Bombadil is, and she replies "He is" this provoked an angry fan into writing Tolkien saying that he was implying that Bombadil was God. God is described as "He is". Of course, Tolkien then wrote him a letter back flatly stating that Bombadil is not God and the comment shouldn't have been taken like that. Nonetheless its an interesting phrase when its use crops up. You know: "We have been sent by He who is called I AM!" "Yeah right" "Worked for Moses...." etc. etc. well I was thinking back on an accurate quote trying to summarize Rei from within the series, only to think of Misato's line in episode 17 when talking about the Eva Pilots: when trying to describe Rei, she just fails for words and says "Rei....is Rei" Could "Rei...is Rei" be taken as another reference to her demi-goddess status within the storyline?

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Oct 12, 2008 5:49 pm That sort of remark is typical of any unusual/nigh-unique/idiosyncratic character.

[]: Re: Possible god-reference: "Rei is Rei"?             Quote Post by AnnieZ » Sun Oct 12, 2008 6:09 pm In the LOTR universe, no the hell knows who is TOM. Because TOM is Tom. An unique character, Tom is Tom because no one know who he is. He is mysterious guy of unknow origins, that's what makes him special. Rei is Rei. No creature walking on the planet is like Rei. Besides Kaworu, lately. We can't compare or define Rei with something that we know. For ex. Rei is like a girl that i meet, Rei is like Shinji or Asuka. Rei is Rei, it is the only way of define her, because we can't define her otherwise. Rei is a very singular being. Being so singular, special and unique made her definition: Rei and Rei, and that's what makes her special, just like Tom. Tom isn't an elf, an human or a demon. No one knows what Tom is. He's a singular creature, he is... JUST TOM. :P That's the way i think.... It's nothing to do with God. Unless. Since God is an unique creature too, we are unable to describe him because God is God, and there is nothing more to say about. God is as he is and there isn't another. [i'm agnostic, i am talking about how the catholics think about God]. But Rei isn't GOD. Rei is Rei is a way of intensify her singularity.

[]: Re: Possible god-reference: "Rei is Rei"?             Quote Post by pat457 » Mon Oct 13, 2008 4:32 am If I might add, Tom Bombadil himself is an intentional enigma, in Tolkien's own words. Also: J.R.R. Tolkien wrote: Tom is not an important person - to the narrative. I suppose he has some importance as a 'comment'. I suppose he has some importance as a 'comment.' I mean, I do not really write like that: he is just an invention (who first appeared in The Oxford Magazine about 1933), and he represents something that I feel important, though I would not be prepared to analyse the feeling precisely. I would not, however, have left him in, if he did not have some kind of function.

[]:             Quote Post by Dartz » Mon Oct 13, 2008 5:13 am "God is" means exactly that. God is. He is not anything, he is not everything, he just is. It's a hard concept to put into words, beyond saying it's a really deep way of saying God exists and continues to exist and God will always exist in the present tense. I think I get it, but I cant really get it out in plain English. "Rei is Rei" also means exactly that. There is nothing else like Rei, she is weird, fey and otherworldly and generally defiant of pigeonholing as anything but Rei Ayanami.

[]:             Quote Post by Lucretius » Mon Oct 13, 2008 7:35 am The katakana used to write "Rei" look just like the Tetragrammaton. No, not really.

[]:             Quote Post by AnnieZ » Mon Oct 13, 2008 7:49 am Like Tom Bombadil itself, NGE is full of enigmas, without them we weren't here i guess.... NGE IS NGE How i can describe with other words?

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Mon Oct 13, 2008 11:00 am Ed wrote: Ed is Ed!

[]:             Quote Post by Action_Bastard » Mon Oct 13, 2008 11:03 am Bow before your new goddess, puny cowboys.

[]:             Quote Post by AnnieZ » Mon Oct 13, 2008 11:29 am BobBQ wrote: Ed wrote: Ed is Ed!

[]:             Quote Post by AchtungAffen » Tue Oct 14, 2008 12:18 pm I believe the God quote has more to do with philosophical concepts. He is the one who is, has the "being", the "to be". Everything that "is", then, comes from that first principle, God, the Verb.
